revision.text:
[[ja:伊波止和気神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwato Wake Shrine | native_name = 伊波止和気神社 | image = 伊波止和気神社 DQqmPVZUIAAeHDk.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwato Wake Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.101403|140.528311}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Iwato Wake Shrine'''|伊波止和気神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11658590|ltq=Q11658590|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P156: {{ill|Q135039980|ltq=Q135039980|qid=Q135039980|1=Shirakahano Shrine}} ** P155: {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|zh|都都古別神社|qid=Q11643869|1=Tsutsukowake Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1205868|ltq=Q1205868|qid=Q1205868|1=Furudono, Fukushima}} * {{ill|Q907389|ltq=Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.101403|140.528311}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * 伊波止和気神社 DQqmPVZUIAAeHDk.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182039 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いわとわけじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5380005005349 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県石川郡古殿町大字田口字戸神269', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '福島県石川郡古殿町戸神', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊波止和気神社]] {{wikidata link|Q48754475}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Iwatowake Shrine |Image = [[Image:Iwatowake Shrine DQqmPVZUIAAeHDk.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District|Q1155869|qid=Q1155869|1=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Furudono Town|Q1205868|qid=Q1205868|1=Furudono, Fukushima}} Ōaza Taguchi, Aza Togami 269 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Togakushi Ōkami
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajikarao-no-Kami}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Togakushi Myōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 15|Q2846|qid=Q2846|1=September 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Iwatowake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Iwatowake Shrine''' (いわとわけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District|Q1155869|qid=Q1155869|1=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Furudono Town|Q1205868|qid=Q1205868|1=Furudono, Fukushima}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === [[File:Sekisan Omotego.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Mount Sekisan (the mountain in the center of the photo; the former site is at its foot)}}]] === Overview === The date of founding is unknown, but a former site exists at the foot of Mount Sekisan in the Sekibe area of {{ill|lt=Shirakawa City|Q750587|qid=Q750587|1=Shirakawa, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. This area is close to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shirakawa Barrier|1=UNKNOWN}}, and nearby place names such as former gold mine sites, “{{ill|lt=Iwaki Kanayama Station|Q11587877|qid=Q11587877|lt=Kanayama|1=Iwaki-Kaneyama Station}},” and “Kogane River” remain. It is therefore thought that in ancient times the shrine functioned as a guardian deity of the checkpoint and the gold mines.
In earlier times the shrine appears to have prospered; during the {{ill|lt=Ōwa era|Q1324768|qid=Q1324768|1=Ōwa}} ({{ill|lt=961|Q23881|qid=Q23881|1=961}}–{{ill|lt=964|Q23951|qid=Q23951|1=964}}), there was a {{ill|lt=horse ground|Q24887670|qid=Q24887670|1=horse field}}, and {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} (mounted archery) was dedicated. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known as “Togakushi Daimyōjin.” Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|qid=Q23469|1=843}}), 9th month, 5th day: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, Merit Rank Nine (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'')''Shoku Nihon Kōki'', Jōwa 10 (843), 9th month, day of Kanoe-Tora (5th) entry (see {{Harvnb|Shintō and Shrine Historical Source Compendium}}). — recorded as “Iwatowake Tenjin.” === Grounds === There is a massive stone called the “Kasa-ishi.” It is said to have originally been located in the Kyūryū River and was moved into the shrine grounds due to river course changes. File:Iwatowake Shrine Haiden DQqmzQ-U8AACSgM.jpg|Worship hall File:Iwatowake Shrine Honden Cover DQqnB LUIAEsB8A.jpg|Main hall protective structure File:Iwatowake Shrine Kasa-ishi DQqsQFDU8AAVwt-.jpg|Kasa-ishi === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/mutu/bun/mut280102-01.html |title=Iwatowake Shrine |date=20181009064739}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwato Wake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwato Wake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwato Wake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Furutone]] [[Category:History of Furutone]]
